title: PureHealth local procurement spend tops $1.7bn - Arabian Business: Latest News on the Middle East, Real
summary: The group said its ICV strategy is focused on strengthening local supply chains, supporting national industries and expanding the role of UAE-based suppliers in healthcare procurement
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
PureHealth has exceeded AED 6.33 billion ($1.72 billion) in local procurement spend since joining the UAE Ministry of Industry and Advanced Technology’s National In-Country Value programme in 2022.

The healthcare group announced the milestone during Make it in the Emirates 2026, as it reported a near threefold increase from AED2.2 billion in 2025.

PureHealth remains on track to meet its AED13 billion ICV commitment by 2032, supported by procurement activity across its healthcare network and supply chain.

The group said its ICV strategy is focused on strengthening local supply chains, supporting national industries and expanding the role of UAE-based suppliers in healthcare procurement.

Rashed AlQubaisi, Group Chief Operating Officer of PureHealth, said: “Our ICV strategy is central to how we scale impact across the healthcare ecosystem. Surpassing AED6.33 billion in local procurement reflects the scale at which we are embedding ICV across our operations, from investing in local suppliers and building national capabilities to enhancing sourcing, logistics, and delivery. By consistently prioritising local capability, we are strengthening supply chain resilience, safeguarding continuity of care, and aligning with the UAE’s industrial agenda while contributing to long-term economic growth and diversification.”

PureHealth’s ICV performance spans several group entities, including SEHA, Sheikh Shakhbout Medical City, Daman, PureLab and Rafed.

SEHA recorded an ICV score of 81.13 per cent, according to PureHealth.

The company said localisation has been embedded across procurement, operations and workforce development.

SEHA received the ICV Champion Award at Make it in the Emirates 2026, while Rafed received the same award in 2025 for its role in supporting SME participation through the programme.

Daman also received three awards from the federal Emiratisation initiative Nafis, including first place for efforts supporting Emiratisation and a Diamond Category award for staff empowerment.

PureHealth’s procurement strategy covers medical supplies, pharmaceuticals, technology and infrastructure, with a focus on UAE-based suppliers that meet global standards.

The group said it is working through long-term partnerships and supplier development programmes to expand opportunities for local businesses and support supply chain resilience.
